summary: Use previous race-lap PitStop counts to capture field-level pit waves that may trigger next-lap reactions.
rationale: Pit decisions are often clustered by race phase and competitor behavior, and the run included past-only race context ideas. Previous-lap pit-wave features are safer than current/future sequence summaries and can reflect undercut/cover dynamics.
implementation_hint: Aggregate PitStop counts and rates by Year-Race-LapNumber, shift these aggregates forward by one and two laps within each race, and map them to current rows. Add interactions with current position, tyre-pressure, and next-stop-feasibility features.
expected_effect: Small AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: Using current-lap or future-lap pit-wave counts changes the interpretation. For a strictly causal version, use only prior race-lap aggregates; test current-lap leave-one-out separately.
